text.title: Stop and pause conditions
text.summary: Four conditions stop or pause the loop: operator halt, irreversible-action gate, Phase 6 full pass, and cron expiry. The orchestrator never decides on its own to terminate; it stops on a defined trigger and records the stop reason in `state.md`.

section.title: Operator halt (immediate)
section.p: Trigger: operator says "stop", "pause", "park", or any equivalent signal in chat or in `state.md`.

Action: the orchestrator stops dispatching, lets in-flight subagents finish (does not abort them), records the halt reason and timestamp in `state.md`, and exits. The next cron tick observes the halt and no-ops.

rule.statement: Operator halt MUST be honored on the same tick it is observed. The orchestrator does NOT defer the halt to the next phase boundary.
rule.rationale: Deferring the halt lets the orchestrator commit work the operator wanted to stop. Same-tick honoring matches the operator's expectation that "stop" means now.

section.title: Irreversible-action gate
section.p: Trigger: the next dispatched action is a deploy, a public push, a paid API key rotation, a DNS change, or any other irreversible / shared-state mutation.

Action: the orchestrator pauses before the action, requests operator approval via the platform user-choice primitive with 2-3 options (proceed, defer, abort), and resumes only on `proceed`. On `defer`, the action is parked in `state.md` and the orchestrator continues with orthogonal work.
section.reference: Phase 5 deploy gate uses this pattern.

section.title: Phase 6 full pass (success terminal state)
section.p: Trigger: every Phase 6 validation returns PASS and `state.md` records `phase: shipped`.

Action: the orchestrator stops driving. The cron tick observes `phase: shipped` and no-ops. The operator can re-engage by editing `state.md` or by invoking a sibling workflow (e.g. `improver` for session capture).

section.title: Cron expiry
section.p: Trigger: the cron job's session-scoped expiry (typically 7 days) is reached and the orchestrator's wake-up signal is no longer scheduled.
Action: the orchestrator records the expiry in `state.md`. The operator decides whether to revive with a longer-lived schedule (`durable: true` or equivalent) or to retire the project.

rule.statement: Every stop MUST be recorded in `state.md` with: ISO timestamp, stop reason (one of: operator-halt, irreversible-gate-defer, phase-6-shipped, cron-expiry), and (for operator halt) the operator's stated reason if provided.
rule.rationale: Future sessions need to know why the loop stopped to decide whether to resume. An unrecorded stop forces the next session to re-read the entire `state.md` log to infer cause — slow and unreliable.

section.title: What is NOT a stop condition
section.p: The following are NOT stop conditions and the orchestrator MUST NOT treat them as such.

list.item: A single subagent failure (handled by the failure-modes recovery path).
Quota exhaustion (handled by the quota-check-time recovery path).
An open question parked in `state.md` (handled by the open-question recovery path; orthogonal work continues).
A pre-commit hook failure (handled by fix-and-retry; never bypass with `--no-verify`).
